files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ActiveStructureCodeDictionaries
files.Summary: Provides access to the structure code dictionaries with the active structure codes.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.AddOn
files.Summary: Represents an add-on, which is a beam modifying device that is inserted into a beam in an accessory slot of the external beam machine. An add-on is used to shape the beam or modulate its intensity or both. Add-ons are blocks, MLCs, wedges, compensators, applicators, a tray, and other devices or materials that can be fixed to a tray to be mounted into an accessory slot.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.AddOnMaterial
files.Summary: Add-on material describes the dosimetric and physical properties of the metal alloy or other substance used to create the add-on.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ApiDataObject
files.Summary: The base class of objects in the Eclipse Scripting API.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Application
files.Summary: The main application interface.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ApplicationPackage
files.Summary:
Presents the application package information in the system.

Note: not all methods are necessarily Published at the moment.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ApplicationScript
files.Summary:
Presents the application script information in the system.

The location of the script file is not stored in the system.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ApplicationScriptLog
files.Summary: The log entry of the application script execution.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Applicator
files.Summary: An applicator add-on, either an electron applicator or cone applicator.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Beam
files.Summary: Represents one beam (also referred to as "field") of an external beam treatment plan. See the definition of DICOM RT Beam for more details.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.BeamCalculationLog
files.Summary: Represents a beam calculation log.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.BeamDose
files.Summary: Represents a dose that is connected to a Beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.BeamParameters
files.Summary: An editable copy of the parameters of a treatment beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.BeamUncertainty
files.Summary: Access to beam uncertainty.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Block
files.Summary: Represents a block add-on, a custom-made beam collimating material fixed to a tray, used to shape the beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Bolus
files.Summary: Represents a bolus, which is custom-made material that is usually fixed to the patient's skin for treatment. The bolus is used to modulate the depth dose profile of a beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.BrachyFieldReferencePoint
files.Summary: This object links a Brachy field to a reference point.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.BrachyPlanSetup
files.Summary: Represents a brachytherapy treatment plan.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.BrachySolidApplicator
files.Summary: Represents a brachytherapy solid applicator part, such as a tandem or ovoid in a Fletcher Suit Delclos (FSD) applicator set. This class holds only the metadata related to the solid applicator part, and links to the that are part of the solid applicator.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.BrachyTreatmentUnit
files.Summary: Represents a brachytherapy afterloader.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.CalculateBrachy3DDoseResult
files.Summary: Brachy 3D dose calculation result

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Calculation.Algorithm
files.Summary: Algorithm

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Calculation.CalculationModel
files.Summary: Calculation Model

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Calculation
files.Summary: Contains a calculation specific functions

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.CalculationResult
files.Summary: Holds the result of the calculation (pass/fail).

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Catheter
files.Summary: Represents a brachytherapy catheter or an applicator channel centerline. Catheters are associated with a , a , and possibly a of the they belong to. Most notably, catheters encapsulate the of the applicator channel centerline and its .

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Compensator
files.Summary: Represents a beam compensator add-on, a custom-made beam modulating material fixed to a tray, used to modulate the beam's intensity.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ControlPoint
files.Summary: Represents a point in a planned sequence of treatment beam parameters. See the definition of control points in a DICOM RT Beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ControlPointCollection
files.Summary: Represents a collection of machine parameters that describe the planned treatment beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ControlPointParameters
files.Summary: An editable copy of the parameters of a control point.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Course
files.Summary: A course represents the course of treatment that a patient will be given. Every patient must have a course, and all plans always belong to a course.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.CustomScriptExecutable
files.Summary: A factory class for creating an application object for a custom script executable.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.DVHData
files.Summary: Represents Dose Volume Histogram (DVH) data.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.DVHEstimationModelStructure
files.Summary: Structure of a DVH estimation model in Planning Model Library

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.DVHEstimationModelSummary
files.Summary: A summary of an DVH Estimation Model. Contains the needed information for selecting a model.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Department
files.Summary: Represents a department in hospital.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Diagnosis
files.Summary: Represents a diagnosis of the patient.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Dose
files.Summary: Represents a 3D dose grid.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.DynamicWedge
files.Summary: A Dynamic Wedge is formed by a moving jaw of a standard collimator during irradiation.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ESAPIActionPackAttribute
files.Summary: Specifies the assembly as an Eclipse visual scripting action pack. Action packs are ESAPI scripts that are used by visual scripts.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ESAPIScriptAttribute
files.Summary: Specifies the assembly as an Eclipse Scripting API (ESAPI) script.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.EnergyMode
files.Summary: Represents an energy mode.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.EnhancedDynamicWedge
files.Summary: An Enhanced Dynamic Wedge is similar to a Dynamic Wedge, but it features more wedge angles than a simple Dynamic Wedge.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Equipment
files.Summary: Provides access to clinical devices and accessories.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.EstimatedDVH
files.Summary: Represents an estimated Dose Volume Histogram (DVH) curve.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.EvaluationDose
files.Summary: Represents an evaluation dose that is connected to a plan that has no beams.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ExternalBeamTreatmentUnit
files.Summary: Represents a treatment machine used for delivering external beam radiotherapy.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ExternalPlanSetup
files.Summary: Represents an external beam plan. For more information, see the definition of the DICOM RT Plan.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.FieldReferencePoint
files.Summary: This object links a treatment beam to a reference point.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Globals
files.Summary: This class is internal to the Eclipse Scripting API.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Hospital
files.Summary: Represents a hospital.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IDVHEstimationCalculator
files.Summary: Interface to the calculation of the DVH Estimates

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Image
files.Summary: Represents a 2D or 3D image, which can be a DRR, a CT, MR, or other volumetric dataset.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonBeam
files.Summary: Proton beam interface.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonBeamParameters
files.Summary: An editable copy of the parameters of a proton beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonControlPoint
files.Summary: Proton control point interface.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonControlPointCollection
files.Summary: Represents a collection of machine parameters that describe the planned proton beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonControlPointPair
files.Summary: An editable copy of a control point pair (the pair of the start control point with an even index, and the end control point with an odd index).

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonControlPointPairCollection
files.Summary: A collection of editable copies of control point pairs that describe the planned proton beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonControlPointParameters
files.Summary: An editable copy of the parameters of a proton control point.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonPlanSetup
files.Summary: Represents a proton treatment plan.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonSpot
files.Summary: The proton scanning spot interface that contains the 3D spot position and spot weight.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonSpotCollection
files.Summary: Interface for the proton scanning spot list.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonSpotParameters
files.Summary: Interface for the proton scanning spot that contains the 3D spot position and spot weight.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.IonSpotParametersCollection
files.Summary: Interface for the editable proton scanning spot list.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Isodose
files.Summary: Represents an isodose level for a fixed absolute or relative dose value.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.LateralSpreadingDevice
files.Summary: The lateral spreading device.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.LateralSpreadingDeviceSettings
files.Summary: Settings for the lateral spreading device.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.MLC
files.Summary: Represents a Multileaf Collimator (MLC) add-on.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.MotorizedWedge
files.Summary: A motorized wedge is a standard wedge placed in the beam for a user-defined fraction of the total treatment time.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OmniWedge
files.Summary: An OmniWedge is a special type of wedge that combines an open field, a motorized wedge, and a virtual wedge to create the desired wedge effect.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationEUDObjective
files.Summary:
A gEUD objective is an exact, upper or lower objective. An exact gEUD objective defines an exact dose value that a target structure should receive. An upper gEUD objective defines the maximum dose value that a structure should receive. A lower gEUD objective defines the minimum dose value that a target structure should receive.

Generalized Equivalent Uniform Dose (gEUD) is a uniform dose that, if delivered over the same number of fractions, yields the same radiobiological effect as the non-uniform dose distribution of interest.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationExcludeStructureParameter
files.Summary: Structures that have this parameter are excluded from the optimization.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationIMRTBeamParameter
files.Summary: Beam-specific optimization parameter for IMRT optimization.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationJawTrackingUsedParameter
files.Summary: An optimization parameter for using jaw tracking in VMAT optimization. The parameter exists if OptimizationSetup.UseJawTracking has been set to true.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationLineObjective
files.Summary: A line objective is a collection of point objectives that have the same priority. It is used to limit the dose in a given structure.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationMeanDoseObjective
files.Summary: A mean objective defines the mean dose that should not be exceeded. The mean objective is used to decrease the dose that a structure receives.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationNormalTissueParameter
files.Summary: An optimization parameter for the normal tissue objective.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationObjective
files.Summary: Provides a common base type for all structure-specific optimization objectives.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationParameter
files.Summary: Provides a common base type for all optimization parameters.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationPointCloudParameter
files.Summary:
Structure-specific parameter for point cloud optimization.

Relevant if the optimization algorithm uses a point cloud. The point cloud parameters are automatically created with default values when you add other structure-specific parameters or objectives in the optimization setup.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationPointObjective
files.Summary: A point objective is either an upper or lower objective. An upper objective is used to limit the dose in a given structure. A lower objective is used to define the desired dose levels in target structures.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationSetup
files.Summary: Gives access to the optimization parameters and objectives.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizationVMATAvoidanceSectors
files.Summary: Beam-specific optimization parameter for VMAT Avoidance sectors.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizerDVH
files.Summary: Contains a structure-specific Dose Volume Histogram (DVH) curve generated in optimization.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizerObjectiveValue
files.Summary: The optimizer objective function value for the structure.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.OptimizerResult
files.Summary: Holds the result of the optimization (pass/fail).

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Patient
files.Summary: Represents a patient.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.PatientSummary
files.Summary: Basic information about the patient.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.PatientSupportDevice
files.Summary: Represents a proton patient support device.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.PlanSetup
files.Summary: Represents a treatment plan. See the definition of a DICOM RT Plan for more information.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.PlanSum
files.Summary: A plan sum describes the cumulative dose summation of several treatment plans. It can be used, for example, to evaluate the dose the patient received from a treatment plan and boost plan together.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.PlanSumComponent
files.Summary: Represents a component plan of a plan sum.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.PlanTreatmentSession
files.Summary: Plan in the treatment session.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.PlanUncertainty
files.Summary: Provides access to Plan Uncertainty parameters. For more information, see Eclipse Photon and Electron Instructions for Use.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.PlanningItem
files.Summary: Common properties of a treatment plan and a plan sum.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.PlanningItemDose
files.Summary: Represents a dose that is connected to a plan setup or a plan sum.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ProtocolPhaseMeasure
files.Summary: Represents the plan measures (quality indices) of the clinical protocol.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ProtocolPhasePrescription
files.Summary: Represents the prescriptions (plan objectives) of the clinical protocol.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RTPrescription
files.Summary: Represents a prescription.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RTPrescriptionConstraint
files.Summary:
Represents a coverage constraint for an RT prescription.

Type of the constraintApplies toExampleValue1Unit1Value2Unit2
TargetsMinimum dose > 95% (of the target dose).95%nullnull
TargetsMaximum dose < 102% (of the target dose).102%nullnull
Organs at riskMaximum dose < 50 Gy.50Gynullnull
TargetsAt least 95% of structure gets 98% of the target dose, i.e. V98% > 95%.98%95%
TargetsNo more than 95% of structure gets 102% of the target dose, i.e. V102% < 95%.102%95%
Organs at riskMaximum mean dose < 45 Gy.45Gynullnull
Organs at riskFree text constraint (a < b).anullbnull

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RTPrescriptionOrganAtRisk
files.Summary: Represents an organ at risk structure.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RTPrescriptionTarget
files.Summary: Represents a prescription target.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RTPrescriptionTargetConstraints
files.Summary: Represents target structure constraints.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RadioactiveSource
files.Summary: Represents a radioactive source installed into a or a seed of a .

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RadioactiveSourceModel
files.Summary: The radioactive source model represents the details of the radioactive source used in brachytherapy. It encapsulates the source isotope, dimensions, and dose calculation parameters.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RangeModulator
files.Summary: The range modulator device.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RangeModulatorSettings
files.Summary: Range modulator settings.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RangeShifter
files.Summary: The range shifter device.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.RangeShifterSettings
files.Summary: Range shifter settings.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ReferencePoint
files.Summary: A reference point associated with a patient.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Registration
files.Summary: Represents the spatial registration matrix between two frames of reference.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ScriptContext
files.Summary: Contains the runtime context information of the active application for the script.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.ScriptEnvironment
files.Summary: Contains the runtime information of the application environment for the script.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.SearchBodyParameters
files.Summary: Parameters for the Search Body feature.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.SeedCollection
files.Summary: Represents a collection of brachytherapy . Each position is also associated with a radioactive source.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.SegmentVolume
files.Summary: The volumetric representation of a structure. This object is used when defining margins for structures, or when performing Boolean operations on structures.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.SerializableObject
files.Summary: Base class for objects that can be serialized.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Series
files.Summary: A series is a collection of radiation therapy objects of a patient. The series is part of a study. See the definition of a DICOM Series for more information.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Slot
files.Summary: A slot is the location (typically on the collimator head of the gantry) where an add-on, such as a wedge or block, is mounted.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.SourcePosition
files.Summary: Represents a brachytherapy source dwell position in a or a seed position in a .

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.StandardWedge
files.Summary: A standard wedge is a physical piece of material with an angle that is static during treatment.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Structure
files.Summary: A structure is a geometrical representation of an anatomical organ, a treatment volume, a marker, or a support structure. See the definition of a DICOM Structure for more information.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.StructureCode
files.Summary: Represents a structure code and its coding scheme.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.StructureCodeDictionary
files.Summary: Represents a set of structure codes as defined by a structure code scheme. The class exposes the structure codes that are available through the implemented interface. Structure code schemes are managed in RT Administration.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.StructureSet
files.Summary: A structure set is a container for structures of a patient, including anatomical organs, treatment volumes and markers, and support structures.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Study
files.Summary: A study is a collection of series.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Technique
files.Summary: Treatment technique used for a beam. Can be, for example, static or arc, or (for proton beams) modulated scanning.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.TradeoffExplorationContext
files.Summary: Context for performing multi-criteria optimization (MCO) to explore different trade-offs.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.TradeoffObjective
files.Summary: Trade-off objective interface that consists of a set of optimization objectives.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.TradeoffPlanGenerationIntermediateDoseMode
files.Summary: Enumeration that specifies the use of intermediate dose when optimizing a plan collection for trade-off exploring.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Tray
files.Summary: A tray add-on is a plate where blocks, compensators, and other beam modifying materials can be fixed to. The tray is inserted into a slot during the treatment of a beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.TreatmentPhase
files.Summary: Treatment phase.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.TreatmentSession
files.Summary: Treatment session.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.TreatmentUnitOperatingLimit
files.Summary: Describes the limits of a treatment unit parameter and provides descriptive information related to it.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.TreatmentUnitOperatingLimits
files.Summary: Provides operating limit information for treatment unit parameters.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.TypeBasedIdValidator
files.Summary: A utility class for validating the data object identifier.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.User
files.Summary: Represents a user.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.Wedge
files.Summary: A wedge is a beam modulating add-on that modifies the dose intensity over all or a part of a treatment beam.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ApplicationScriptApprovalStatus
files.Summary: The approval statuses of the application script.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ApplicationScriptType
files.Summary: The type of the application script.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ApprovalHistoryEntry
files.Summary: An entry in the plan approval history.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ArcOptimizationAperture
files.Summary: Arc optimization aperture defined as an abstract rectangle around the isocenter in cm. Named according to IEC61217 but independent of any physical jaw collimators. ///

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.AxisAlignedMargins
files.Summary: Represents margins aligned to the axes of the image coordinate system, in mm. Negative margins are not allowed, but it is possible to specify whether the margins represent an inner or outer margin.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.BeamNumber
files.Summary: Represents a unique identifier for a beam of a plan. The identifier is unique within the scope of the plan.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.BeamTechnique
files.Summary: An enumeration of beam techniques.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.BlockType
files.Summary: A type flag that tells whether a block is an aperture block or a shielding block. An aperture block is used to limit the radiated area while a shielding block is made to protect a sensitive organ.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.BrachyTreatmentTechniqueType
files.Summary: The enumeration of Brachytherapy treatment techniques.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.CalculationType
files.Summary: Calculation type.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ChangeBrachyTreatmentUnitResult
files.Summary: Return value for method .

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ClinicalGoal
files.Summary: Represents a clinical goal.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ClosedLeavesMeetingPoint
files.Summary: Specifies where the closed MLC leaf pairs are parked in an MLC leaf fit operation. Bank_One: Varian = B, IEC MLCX = X1, IEC MLCY = Y1; Bank_Two: Varian = A, IEC MLCX = X2, IEC MLCY = Y2

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.CourseClinicalStatus
files.Summary: Clinical Status of Course

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DRRCalculationParameters
files.Summary: Parameters for DRR calculation.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DVHEstimateType
files.Summary: Represents the type of a DVH estimate curve

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DVHEstimationStructureType
files.Summary: Structure type as defined in Planning Model Library: PTV or OAR

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DVHPoint
files.Summary: Represents a value on a Dose Volume Histogram (DVH) curve.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DoseProfile
files.Summary: Represents a dose profile.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DoseValue.DoseUnit
files.Summary: The unit of the dose value.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DoseValue
files.Summary: Represents a dose value. DoseValue semantics follows the semantics of System.Double, with DoseValue.Undefined corresponding to Double.NaN.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DoseValueDisplayConfig
files.Summary: Configure the settings related to the dose value display for the application. Defaults to the same settings as Eclipse.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DoseValuePresentation
files.Summary: Types of presentation for dose values.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.DosimeterUnit
files.Summary: The dosimeter unit.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ExternalBeamMachineParameters
files.Summary: The parameters for the external beam treatment unit.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.FitToStructureMarginType
files.Summary: Margin type

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.FitToStructureMargins
files.Summary: Margins that are used when fitting a field device to a structure from the BEV perspective

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.Fluence
files.Summary: Represents the fluence for a beam. The resolution in the fluence matrix is 2.5 mm in x and y directions. In the fluence matrix, x dimension is the number of columns, and y dimension is the number of rows.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.GantryDirection
files.Summary: The enumeration of gantry rotation directions.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.GoalEvalResult
files.Summary: Clinical Goal Evaluation Result

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.GoalPriority
files.Summary: Clinical Goal Priority

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.IDoseValueDisplaySettings
files.Summary: Application specific dose value display settings.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ImageApprovalHistoryEntry
files.Summary: An entry in the image approval history.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ImageApprovalStatus
files.Summary: The enumeration of image approval statuses.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ImageProfile
files.Summary: Represents an image line profile.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ImagingBeamSetupParameters
files.Summary: Setup parameters for imaging fields.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.ImagingSetup
files.Summary: Set of available imaging setups.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.IonBeamScanMode
files.Summary: The method of beam scanning to be used during treatment. Used with IonBeams.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.IonOptimizationIntermediateDoseOption
files.Summary: Options for using intermediate dose in proton optimization.

files.Title: VMS.TPS.Common.Model.Types.IonPlanNormalizationParameters
